success_surface: Comprehensive pre-task exploration combined with dual-requirement fulfillment (formula + formatting)
effective_behaviors: Thoroughly inspect spreadsheet structure before coding
Verify actual header locations vs assumptions from preview
Use openpyxl when formulas and formatting must coexist
Implement dynamic formulas instead of static values
Handle empty/edge cases explicitly in formulas
Apply formatting operations on same pass as formula insertion
Ensure output directory exists before saving
evidence: Agent loaded workbook first and printed sheet names, dimensions, headers, sample data, and target area status
Discovered headers were in Row 2 not Row 1 from initial preview, preventing misaligned formula ranges
Used openpyxl throughout for both cell value (formulas) and style (fill color) modifications
Created IF(INDEX(MATCH())) formulas returning empty strings when search input blank
Applied PatternFill to target cells A20-B26 in same code block as formula insertion
Created output directory with os.makedirs(exist_ok=True) before wb.save()

memory_items.title: Pre-Coding Spreadsheet Audit
memory_items.lesson: Always explore full workbook structure before implementing changes
memory_items.generalization: Load workbook, verify all sheet names, check actual header rows, sample data rows, inspect target area for existing content, and identify all relevant column mappings by content matching rather than assumptions from limited previews.

memory_items.title: Formula + Formatting Tool Selection
memory_items.lesson: When task requires both dynamic calculations and visual styling, use openpyxl exclusively
memory_items.generalization: pandas.to_excel loses styles and converts formulas to values. For any task combining formula insertion with formatting changes, load with openpyxl.load_workbook(), modify Workbook objects, then save directly.

memory_items.title: Robust Formula Pattern for User Input Areas
memory_items.lesson: Wrap lookup formulas with conditional checks for empty inputs
memory_items.generalization: When creating formulas that react to user-typed cells, wrap INDEX/MATCH or VLOOKUP with IF(condition="", "", ... ) to return empty string when source cell is blank, preventing #N/A errors and maintaining clean appearance.

memory_items.title: Consolidated Operation Passes
memory_items.lesson: Apply multiple related modifications in single iteration loop
memory_items.generalization: When modifying multiple cell attributes (values, styles, merged ranges), perform all operations within one row/column loop rather than separate passes, reducing I/O overhead and ensuring consistency.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Pre-Coding Audit Requirement
- Always load and inspect full workbook structure before implementing changes
- Verify actual header row positions (may differ from initial preview)
- Sample data rows to confirm data boundaries and formats
- Check target area for existing content or conflicts
- Identify columns by content matching, not hardcoded indices

### Dual-Purpose Operations (Formulas + Formatting)
- When formulas AND styles are required, use openpyxl exclusively throughout
- Never combine pandas for data with openpyxl for styles—use openpyxl for both
- Insert formulas via .value = 'Excel Formula' syntax
- Apply styles via Cell.fill property before saving
- Perform all cell modifications in consolidated operation passes

### Input-Sensitive Formula Patterns
- Wrap lookup formulas with IF(source="", "", ...) to handle empty user inputs gracefully
- Prevent #N/A errors from propagating in user-facing tables
- Use INDEX/MATCH pairs for flexible bidirectional lookups across columns

### Output Preparation
- Create output directories with makedirs(exist_ok=True) before attempting to save
- Use try-except blocks around wb.save() for error handling
- Print confirmation messages after successful operations
